description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:8 Docker image with two stages: review and deploy. Add a start_review job in the review stage that runs on all branches except master, installs the now CLI via npm, creates a preview URL with `now --public --token=${NOW_TOKEN} -n ${REPO_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}`, strips the protocol using sed, sets an alias to `${REPO_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}.now.sh`, and removes the temporary deployment; it defines a review environment named `review/$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME` with an on_stop link to stop_review. Add a stop_review manual job in the review stage, also limited to branches except master, that installs now and deletes the preview deployment with `now rm -t ${NOW_TOKEN} --yes ${REPO_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}`. Add a start_production job in the deploy stage that runs only on the master branch, performs the same now installation and deployment steps but sets the alias to the production domain `whereisscihub.now.sh` and cleans up the temporary deployment, and defines a production environment pointing to that URL.
